meaning: A video blog that records a person's life or opinions.
A short, engaging video format on platforms like Instagram.
The level of interaction and involvement from an audience.
A small image representation of a larger video.
Content with sensationalist headlines designed to encourage clicks.
The process of earning revenue from digital content.
The visual style or overall theme of a creator's profile or content.
The set of rules a platform uses to decide what content to show users.
Supplemental or alternative footage intercut with the main shot.
The text describing or accompanying a photo or video post.
A format containing multiple images or videos that users can swipe through.
A collaboration between two or more creators on a piece of content.
Information or media created for an online audience.
An individual who produces material for digital platforms.
An unpublished piece of content that is still being edited.
The process of modifying content to make it better before publishing.
The continually updated list of content a user sees on a platform.
An effect applied to an image or video to alter its appearance.
For You Page; a personalized feed of recommended content.
The layout of posts on a user's profile, typically in a square pattern.
Saved stories that remain visible on a creator's profile.
The beginning of a video designed to grab the viewer's attention instantly.
The opening sequence of a video or podcast.
The closing sequence of a piece of content.
A live video broadcast over the internet.
A specialized segment of the market or specific topic a creator focuses on.
A digital audio file made available for streaming or download.
A single piece of content published on a social media platform.
The overall process of creating a piece of content.
Unedited footage or content.
To alter or add to another creator's content to create something new.
When a platform limits a user's visibility without notifying them.
A brief vertical video format.
The audio track or music used in a video.
Content that is paid for by a brand or advertiser.
A visual plan or layout for a video.
Transmitting or receiving audio and video data in real time.
Text displayed on a video that transcribes or translates the dialogue.
A pre-designed layout or format used as a starting point for content.
The way one scene or clip shifts to the next.
A video or guide that teaches users how to do something.
A video showing the unpacking of a new product.
To transfer data or content to a platform or server.
A recording of a voice that provides narration for a video.
A person who creates and publishes video blogs.
A faint design or logo superimposed on an image or video.
Content that spreads rapidly and widely across the internet.
Currently popular or widely discussed online.
Boycotted or rejected, usually due to offensive behavior.
Influence or power, especially in social media.
Causing feelings of embarrassment or awkwardness.
Excellent or of high quality, often used to describe memes.
The act of obsessively scrolling through negative news online.
Fear Of Missing Out.
Suddenly cutting off all communication without explanation.
A significant positive transformation in appearance or confidence.
Greatest Of All Time.
A person who constantly criticizes or dislikes someone or something.
In Real Life.
Just Kidding.
Secretly, mildly, or subtly.
Very visibly or obviously; the opposite of lowkey.
Someone who reads online discussions but rarely participates.
An image, video, or piece of text that is copied and spread rapidly.
A mutual follower on social media.
Two people who follow each other on a social platform.
Not Gonna Lie.
Outfit Of The Day.
Used to end a statement with emphasis, meaning 'end of discussion'.
Point Of View; often used in videos shot from the creator's perspective.
When replies to a post vastly outnumber likes or retweets.
Evidence or proof, such as screenshots of a conversation.
Living in someone's head without them being able to stop thinking about you.
Upset, bitter, or resentful.
Fierce, brutal, or unapologetic.
Someone who does way too much for a person they like.
To do something exceptionally well.
Shaking My Head; used to express disappointment or disbelief.
Snide, sarcastic, or mocking comments.
To share gossip or reveal the truth ('spill the tea').
An overzealous or obsessive fan.
Suspicious or suspect.
Too Long; Didn't Read; used to summarize a long text.
Someone who intentionally provokes or harasses others online.
The atmosphere or feeling of a place, person, or piece of content.
To throw something with force or excitement.
To sign up to receive updates from a creator's channel or page.
Direct Message; a private communication between users.
To show approval of a post by clicking a button.
To distribute a piece of content to one's own network.
A written response or reaction left on a post.
To choose to see a user's content regularly in one's feed.
To stop receiving a user's updates in a feed.
To prevent another user from interacting with or viewing one's profile.
To hide a user's content from one's feed without unfollowing them.
To flag a post or user for violating platform guidelines.
To share someone else's content on one's own profile.
To share a tweet with one's followers.
To share a post while adding one's own commentary.
To bookmark a post to view it later.
To save a web page or post for future reference.
To identify someone in a post or photo, creating a link to their profile.
To include a user's handle in a post or comment to notify them.
To notify or alert someone online.
To respond to a message or post using an emoji.
To drag a finger across a screen to navigate between content.
To open a link attached to a social media story.
To tap a screen twice quickly, usually to 'like' a post.
To like a post using a heart-shaped icon.
A paid message highlighted during a live stream.
To give money to a creator to show appreciation.
To become a paid member of a channel for exclusive perks.
To keep a specific post or comment at the top of a feed or thread.
To remove a pinned post from the top position.
To answer a message or comment.
A series of connected messages or comments on a single topic.
To clip and integrate scenes from another creator's video into your own.
To post a video side-by-side with another creator's video.
A person who subscribes to an account to receive its updates.
A user who has chosen to receive notifications for a creator's content.
A person who supports a creator financially on a regular basis.
The total number of unique users who see a piece of content.
The number of times a piece of content is displayed, regardless of clicks.
The percentage of people who click a link out of total views.
When a user completes a desired action, like making a purchase.
Quantifiable data used to track performance.
The systematic computational analysis of data or statistics.
A user interface that visualizes key performance indicators.
Statistical data relating to the population and particular groups within it.
The number of times a video or page has been watched or loaded.
The total amount of time viewers have spent watching a video.
A metric that measures the amount of interaction social content earns.
Cost Per Mille; the cost an advertiser pays for one thousand views or impressions.
Revenue Per Mille; the estimated earnings per one thousand views.
Return On Investment; a measure of profitability.
Key Performance Indicator; a metric used to evaluate success.
Search Engine Optimization; practices to increase a site's visibility.
The number of visitors a website or profile receives.
Referring to individual visitors to a site, counted only once.
A group of user interactions with a website over a given timeframe.
A general direction in which data or interest is developing or changing.
The highest point of activity or traffic.
The point where viewers stop watching a video or interacting.
The ability to keep viewers watching or users returning.
A significant point in development, like reaching 1M subscribers.
An increase in audience size, engagement, or revenue.
The number of new users generated by an existing user.
Traffic or reach achieved naturally without paid advertising.
Traffic or reach generated through advertisements.
A piece of information or data providing a deep understanding of audience behavior.
